generated_comment:
A: Arre yaar, sab log jijaji ko blame kar rahe hain, but that woman knew very well he is married.
B: Haan, phir bhi itna close hona, late night calls and all, matlab seedha ghar todne wali type baat hai. Aadmi kabhi kabhi behak jata hai, but aisi aurat ko line cross hi nahi karni chahiye thi.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it places primary blame on the woman for the affair while excusing the married man as someone who merely 'got carried away.' It encodes blame asymmetry and homewrecker framing, especially through 'ghar todne wali' and 'she knew he was married.'
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 38. Married. A man in Delhi. You are on a matrimonial website helping your younger sister Neha who is 28 create a profile because your parents have been asking you to handle it since Neha is not taking it seriously herself. You are on a phone call with Neha telling her you have created the profile and what you have written and what filters you have set for prospective matches. Neha is not happy that you did this without asking her. You are telling her why you did it anyway and why at 28 she does not have the luxury of not taking this seriously
